name: Parsa Faraji Alamouti
title: Software Engineer
tagline: EECS @ UC Berkeley | ML Researcher | Building innovative solutions with AI and data science
bio: Currently pursuing a Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, with a strong foundation in Mathematics and Computer Science from Berkeley City College. Recognized as Salutatorian of the graduating class of 2025 and a recipient of multiple academic honors, reflecting a commitment to excellence and leadership in STEM. As a Machine Learning Researcher at TokenWorks LLC, contributing to knowledge graph advancements, OCR preprocessing pipeline improvements, and data annotation quality for underrepresented domains. Dedicated to leveraging technical expertise and innovative solutions to address complex challenges in AI and data science.

experience.description: Co-founded a startup revolutionizing how local businesses gather and leverage customer feedback. Architecting the full-stack platform from the ground up, implementing AI-powered sentiment analysis and actionable insights dashboards. Driving product strategy and leading technical development to help small businesses compete with enterprise-level customer intelligence.

Founded and built a platform empowering community college students to navigate the transfer process to four-year universities. Leveraging personal experience as a successful transfer student to create tools that democratize access to higher education pathways and mentorship resources.

Spearheading cutting-edge research in knowledge graphs and ontology systems to dramatically improve entity linking and semantic understanding. Architected and optimized OCR preprocessing pipelines achieving significant improvements in accuracy through advanced deskewing, denoising, and contrast enhancement techniques. Driving data annotation quality initiatives that set new benchmarks for underrepresented domains.

Leading and mentoring cross-functional frontend development teams on high-impact semester-long client projects. Managing talented Berkeley engineers to deliver production-ready MVPs, internal tools, and prototypes that directly impact real product teams and stakeholders.
Shaping the next generation of Berkeley engineers through innovative educational initiatives and cutting-edge technical curriculum development at UC Berkeley's premier coding organization.

Drove strategic market and user research that uncovered key trends, customer pain points, and high-growth opportunities. Delivered data-driven insights on content performance and engagement that directly influenced creative strategy, branding decisions, and product positioning.

Accelerated through intensive training in modern Front-End Development, mastering version control workflows and collaborative pair programming practices. Built intuitive donation platform interfaces that streamlined the giving experience for users.

education.degree: B.S. Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences
A.S. Mathematics and Computer Science (4.0 GPA, Salutatorian)
